steps.content: Identify the starting value: at sea level ($x = 0$ feet), the boiling point is $212^{\circ}$F. This is the constant term in the equation.

Determine the rate of change: for every 550 feet of altitude, the boiling point decreases by $1^{\circ}$F. The rate is $-\dfrac{1}{550}$ degrees per foot, so after $x$ feet the total decrease is $\dfrac{x}{550}$ degrees.

Evaluate all choices:
  • $B = 550 + \frac{x}{212}$: $B = 550 + \dfrac{x}{212}$ — the constants are swapped and the sign is wrong; boiling point would increase with altitude. Eliminated.
  • $B = 550 - \frac{x}{212}$: $B = 550 - \dfrac{x}{212}$ — constants are swapped entirely; starts at 550 instead of 212. Eliminated.
  • $B = 212 + \frac{x}{550}$: $B = 212 + \dfrac{x}{550}$ — starts correctly at 212 but adds instead of subtracts, so boiling point would rise with altitude. Eliminated.
  • $B = 212 - \frac{x}{550}$: $B = 212 - \dfrac{x}{550}$ — starts at 212 and decreases by $\dfrac{x}{550}$ for every $x$ feet above sea level. That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
